title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but may introduce risks if improperly configured, used without validation, or if malicious actors exploit its widespread adoption to distribute tampered versions.
recommended_actions: Verify the authenticity and integrity of the tool before downloading and using it.
Conduct internal testing in a controlled environment to ensure the tool does not introduce unintended vulnerabilities.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback for any reported issues or security concerns.

title: A weakness has been identified in Totolink WA300 5.2cu.7112_B20190227. The impacted element is...
description: A weakness has been identified in Totolink WA300 5.2cu.7112_B20190227. The impacted element is the function setLanguageCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component POST Request Handler. This manipulation of the argument langType causes command injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. Upgrading to version 3.6.28.dev2 addresses this issue. The ident

title: A flaw has been found in PrefectHQ prefect up to 3.6.13. Affected is an unknown function of the...
description: A flaw has been found in PrefectHQ prefect up to 3.6.13. Affected is an unknown function of the file /api/events/in of the component WebSocket Endpoint. Executing a manipulation can lead to missing authentication. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been published and may be used. Upgrading to version 3.6.14 is able to address this issue. This patch is called 0d3ab3c2d3f9f98abfafdf7b9f6d4f8ed3925e40. It is recommended to upgrade the affected component.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The flaw in the WebSocket Endpoint of PrefectHQ prefect up to version 3.6.13 allows unauthenticated remote attackers to exploit the system, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade PrefectHQ prefect to version 3.6.14 or later to address the vulnerability.
Review and monitor WebSocket Endpoint activity for any signs of unauthorized access or suspicious behavior.
Implement additional network-level protections, such as IP whitelisting or Web Application Firewalls (WAF), to mitigate potential exploitation.

title: A vulnerability was found in code-projects Gym Management System In PHP and Windows NT 1.0. This...
description: A vulnerability was found in code-projects Gym Management System In PHP and Windows NT 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /index.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument day results in sql injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for SQL injection via the 'day' parameter in the Gym Management System. This could lead to unauthorized access, data leakage, or database manipulation if exploited. Since the exploit is public, the risk of exploitation increases.
recommended_actions: Apply input validation and parameterized queries to sanitize user inputs in the affected code.
Update or patch the Gym Management System to the latest version if a fix is available.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink WA300 5.2cu.7112_B20190227. The affected element...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Totolink WA300 5.2cu.7112_B20190227. The affected element is the function loginauth of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component POST Request Handler. The manipulation of the argument http_host results in buffer overflow. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The buffer overflow vulnerability in Totolink WA300 could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data theft, or disruption of network services.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware of Totolink WA300 devices to the latest version if a patch is available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A flaw has been found in crocodilestick Calibre-Web-Automated up to 4.0.6. Affected by this issue...
description: A flaw has been found in crocodilestick Calibre-Web-Automated up to 4.0.6. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file cps/cwa_functions.py of the component Admin Endpoint. This manipulation causes missing authentication. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through a pull request but has not reacted yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7714.

title: A stack overflow vulnerability exists in the WebCam Server Login functionality of GeoVision GV...
description:
A stack overflow vulnerability exists in the WebCam Server Login functionality of GeoVision GV-VMS V20 20.0.2. A specially crafted HTTP request can lead to an arbitrary code execution. An attacker can make an unauthenticated HTTP request to trigger this vulnerability.

#### Stack-overflow via unconstrained sscanf

The call to `sscanf` at [1] to split the `Buffer` variable into the `username` and `password` variables doesn't limit the size of the extracted content to match the destination buffers

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The stack overflow vulnerability in GeoVision GV-VMS WebCam Server Login functionality allows attackers to execute arbitrary code remotely, potentially leading to full system compromise and unauthorized access to sensitive data.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply patches or updates provided by GeoVision to address CVE-2026-7372.
Implement network-level protections, such as web application firewalls (WAFs), to detect and block malicious HTTP requests targeting this vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit of all systems using GeoVision GV-VMS software and monitor for signs of exploitation or suspicious activity.

title: A vulnerability was determined in Totolink WA300 5.2cu.7112_B20190227. This issue affects the...
description: A vulnerability was determined in Totolink WA300 5.2cu.7112_B20190227. This issue affects the function UploadCustomModule of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component POST Request Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument File can lead to buffer overflow. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink WA300 firmware allows remote attackers to execute a buffer overflow attack via a manipulated POST request. This could lead to unauthorized code execution, system compromise, or denial of service, potentially exposing sensitive data or disrupting operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Totolink WA300 firmware to the latest version if a patch is available from the vendor.
Implement network segmentation and firewall rules to restrict access to the affected device from untrusted networks.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify attempts to exploit this vulnerability.

title: Multiple reflected cross-site scripting (xss) vulnerabilities exist in the Web Interface / ssi...

title: An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of...
description:
An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility 9.0.5. Listening to broadcast packets can lead to credentials leak. An attacker can listen to broadcast messages to trigger this vulnerability.


When interacting with various Geovision devices on the network, the utility may send privileged commands; in order to do so, the username and password of the device need to be provided. In some instances the command is broadcaste

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility 9.0.5 allows attackers to intercept broadcast packets and potentially obtain device credentials. This can lead to unauthorized access, manipulation of device settings, or compromise of sensitive data, especially in environments relying on these devices for security or surveillance.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of the GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility, if available, or contact the vendor for a security update.
Implement network segmentation to isolate GeoVision devices from untrusted networks and limit exposure to potential attackers.
Enable network monitoring to detect and alert on suspicious broadcast packet activity and unauthorized access attempts.

Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices and restrict access to the Web Interface from untrusted networks.
Monitor HTTP traffic for signs of exploitation attempts and implement Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block malicious requests.

title: GV-VMS V20 is a Video Monitoring Software used to gather the feeds of many surveillance cameras...
description:
GV-VMS V20 is a Video Monitoring Software used to gather the feeds of many surveillance cameras and manage other security devices. It is a native application accessed locally, but it is also possible to enable remote access via the "WebCam Server" feature.  Once enabled, it is possible to access to the management and monitoring feature via a regular Web interface. This webersever is another native application, compiled without ASLR, which makes exploitation much easier and more likely. 



Most

description: An os command injection vulnerability exists in the DdnsSetting.cgi functionality of GeoVision LPC2011/LPC2211 1.10. A specially crafted DDNS configuration can lead to arbitrary command execution. An attacker can modify a configuration value to trigger this vulnerability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands on the affected devices, potentially leading to full system compromise, unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or further lateral movement within the network.

Restrict access to the DdnsSetting.cgi functionality by implementing network segmentation and limiting access to trusted IP ranges.
Conduct a thorough review of system logs for signs of exploitation and monitor for unusual activity on devices running the affected firmware.

title: CVE-2026-42363 - An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVis
description:
An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility 9.0.5. Listening to broadcast packets can lead to credentials leak. An attacker can listen to broadcast messages to trigger this vulnerability.


When interacting with various Geovision devices on the network, the utility may send privileged commands; in order to do so, the username and password of the device need to be provided. In some instances the command is broadcasted over UDP and the username/password are encrypted using a cryptographic protocol that appears to be derivated from Blowfish. However the symmetric key used for the encryption is also included in the packet, and thus the security of the username/password only relies on the "obscurity" of the encryption scheme. An attacker on the same LAN can listen to the broadcast traffic once an admin user interacts with the device, and decrypt the credentials using their own implementation of the algorithm. With this password the attacker would have full control over the device configuration, allowing them to change its ip address or even reset it to factory default.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42363.
